validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile on hook + test exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_continue_loop_claude -v passes 5/5 (0.5s); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 178/178 up from 173 (39.6s)

checkpoint.summary: unit_034 complete. Added a fleet-mode pre-check to templates/claude-code/hooks/continue-loop.py: reads execution.fleet.mode with a safe chain (missing fleet block treated as idle for v1 back-compat); if mode != 'idle' ('dispatched' or 'merging') the hook calls _stop() which deletes .invoke-active and exits 0. Sits between 'read state.json' and the existing loop-budget/blockers/open_questions chain. New test_continue_loop_claude.py (5 cases, 2 classes) runs the hook as a subprocess with controlled stdin payloads -- covers dispatched + merging stop cases, idle-falls-through case, missing-fleet-treated-as-idle case, and a positive control with a fully-seeded harness reaching exit 2 (block-continue) to prove the guard does not falsely trigger on idle state. Test suite 173 -> 178.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_035 (PHASE_008): mirror the fleet-mode guard into skills/development-harness/templates/hooks/continue-loop.py (Cursor variant). Same logic adapted to Cursor's hook protocol (return `{}` to stop instead of exit 0; Cursor reads fields from input_data.get('workspace_roots') not 'cwd'). Add test_continue_loop_cursor.py with the same five cases.
